message:
fix: Handle GPT-5.2 role detection and header artifacts

OpenAI's GPT-5.2 release changed the share page DOM structure, using
visible "You said:" and "ChatGPT said:" text headers instead of proper
data-message-author-role attributes. This caused messages to be labeled
as "Other" instead of "User" or "Assistant".

Changes in both CDP and Playwright code paths:

1. Text-based role detection fallback:
   - When data-message-author-role is missing or "unknown", examine the
     first 100 characters of message text for header patterns
   - Use anchored regex /^(?:#{1,6}\s*)?chatgpt\s+said/ to detect assistant
   - Use anchored regex /^(?:#{1,6}\s*)?you\s+said/ to detect user
   - Anchor to start prevents false positives from phrases like
     "I think you said..." appearing in message content

2. Alternating role fallback:
   - Added 'chatgpt' to providers using alternating user/assistant fallback
   - For any remaining 'unknown' roles, alternate starting with 'user'
   - This handles edge cases where text detection also fails

3. Header artifact stripping:
   - After Turndown conversion, strip "### You said:" and similar headers
   - Role information is already captured in the ## User/## Assistant headings
   - Regex: /^#{3,6}\s*(You said|ChatGPT said):?\s*$/gim
   - Collapse resulting triple+ newlines to double newlines

Tested with real GPT-5.2 share links to verify correct role detection
and clean markdown output.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
